behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is primarily a module handoff/orchestration behavior (history builder -> LLM request payload), where unit tests on a trimming helper can miss wiring/ordering mistakes. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real HTTP/subprocess/OS checks or external contract validation. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment, only on payload content. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic sequence required.
behaviors.test_description: Run an integration test that exercises the request pipeline end-to-end in-process: provide >10 prior messages, invoke the chatbot service once, and assert the payload sent by the LLM client contains exactly the most recent 10 messages in correct order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is explicitly output-quality/relevance of model content; deterministic stubs cannot validate whether replies are on-topic. MOCK-HIDE: mocked model outputs would always pass relevance assertions if fixtures are preselected. MULTI-STEP: behavior asks for single response quality, not a chained multi-agentic workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run an agentic evaluation against the real model on a curated prompt set and score whether responses directly answer the asked question (with rubric or human/LLM judge). Gate on relevance thresholds and off-topic rate.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires multiple agentic steps in sequence: (1) initial generation, (2) tone judgment ('too formal'), and (3) regeneration trigger path. Per MULTI-STEP and integration-brake(d), this is not just local orchestration; correctness depends on model decision(s) governing handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real pipeline with casual-tier prompts likely to produce formal drafts. Verify sequence: first draft generated, tone check flags it as too formal, regeneration is invoked, and a second draft is produced. Assert each stage event occurs in order.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can appear at service->repository->schema mapping boundaries. Integration with a real local DB catches missing rows/fields/order. REAL-INFRA/System triad: behavior text does not require real external API/HTTP/subprocess contract checks; local DB round-trip suffices.
behaviors.test_description: Run an integration test with a real local test database: submit a message that causes at least one regeneration, then read back persisted records and assert all exchange items are present with expected linkage and correctly ordered timestamps.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-stage orchestration over LLM-involved steps: initial draft, tone-trigger decision, regenerated draft, and final-response selection. Per integration-brake(d), handoff correctness is governed by agentic decisions in sequence, so Integration would risk false confidence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real end-to-end flow for cases that trigger tone regeneration and assert the user-facing output channel returns only the regenerated response, while the rejected draft is absent from customer-visible payloads but remains only in internal logs if applicable.
